title: Warren Buffett's message to investors: Be careful
excerpt: Warren Buffett called out widespread gambling on stocks and the huge amounts being spent on AI. He also discussed his Alphabet wager.
content_html:
By Theron Mohamed

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Warren Buffett is worried.

The legendary investor called out reckless speculation in markets and the massive cost of the AI buildout during a rare interview with CNBC on Wednesday.

"It's tough to find values when everybody is preferring gambling," the famously disciplined bargain hunter said.

Berkshire Hathaway's 95-year-old chairman, who stepped down as CEO at the turn of the year, said it's been hard to find good deals for years now because the market has shifted in favor of speculators.

He said that "humans love to gamble so much, there's more money in actually cultivating gamblers than there is in cultivating investors."

Business Insider reached out to several of Buffett's close followers for their take on his message. Buffett's secretary didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

"Buffett is warning investors — not to avoid AI, but to be careful when speculation becomes the dominant force setting prices," Adam Schwartz, the chief investor of Black Bear Value Partners, told Business Insider.

The business icon has "always believed that eventually fundamentals matter, even if they don't seem to for long stretches," Schwartz continued, adding that investor capital is "chasing excitement instead of cash flows" in the AI era.

David Kass, a finance professor at the University of Maryland who blogs about Berkshire, told Business Insider that Buffett does seem "concerned" by "troubling" trends in markets such as growing use of derivatives and shrinking time horizons.

But Kass noted that Buffett's "extreme caution" — Berkshire had a record $380 billion cash pile at the end of March — has stopped him from capitalizing on a "sharply rising equity market over the past few years."

The Alphabet puzzle

Buffett flagged during the interview how so-called hyperscalers such as Meta, Microsoft, and Alphabet are spending hundreds of billions of dollars on microchips, data centers, and other infrastructure to win the AI race.

"That's real money," he said. "That's the game they're playing now. They weren't playing that game with computer software."

Buffett seemed "troubled" by those huge outlays, Kass said, likely because they're eroding cash flows that previously funded stock buybacks, compelling the companies to raise external capital, and raising the prospect they'll spend a ton of money with little to show for it.

Yet Buffett also revealed to CNBC that he decided Berkshire should invest in Alphabet last year. Berkshire ramped up that position to nearly 58 million shares during the nine months ended March 31, securing a stake worth $20.5 billion today.

Berkshire — led by Buffett's successor as CEO, Greg Abel — invested another $10 billion in Alphabet in a private placement in June. The unexpected deal boosted the size of its wager to nearly $31 billion at Thursday's close, assuming no changes have been made. That has made Alphabet the third-biggest holding in Berkshire's stock portfolio after Apple and American Express, overtaking Coca-Cola.

"I think they're more likely to be a winner based on the record than probably 90% or 95% of what gets merchandised through Wall Street," Buffett said about Alphabet.

Brett Gardner, the author of "Buffett's Early Investments," told Business Insider he was a "little confused" by Buffett investing in Alphabet, given his concerns about the cost of the AI buildout.

"To make a bet on Google, I think you need to have some sense of the returns on these investments," Gardner said, but "Buffett didn't seem to have a strong view" on that front.

Buffett might think Alphabet can pare its spending if its AI investments aren't paying off, and "return to gushing cash again, which makes it an attractive investment," Gardner said. "I don't know!"

Whatever Buffett sees in Alphabet, he's picking his spots carefully in a red-hot market, and signaling other investors should do the same.

sentences.en: Warren Buffett's close followers say he's worried.

blocks.html_en: Warren Buffett is worried.

sentences.en: Warren Buffett is worried.
sentences.zh: 沃伦·巴菲特很担心。

blocks.html_en: The legendary investor called out reckless speculation in markets and the massive cost of the AI buildout during a rare interview with CNBC on Wednesday.

sentences.en: The legendary investor called out reckless speculation in markets and the massive cost of the AI buildout during a rare interview with CNBC on Wednesday.
sentences.zh: 这位传奇投资者在周三罕见地接受 CNBC 采访时，痛斥了市场上鲁莽的投机行为以及人工智能建设的巨大成本。

blocks.html_en: "It's tough to find values when everybody is preferring gambling," the famously disciplined bargain hunter said.

sentences.en: "It's tough to find values when everybody is preferring gambling," the famously disciplined bargain hunter said.
sentences.zh: “当每个人都喜欢赌博时，很难找到价值，”这位以纪律严明着称的便宜货猎人说。

blocks.html_en: Berkshire Hathaway's 95-year-old chairman, who stepped down as CEO at the turn of the year, said it's been hard to find good deals for years now because the market has shifted in favor of speculators.

sentences.en: Berkshire Hathaway's 95-year-old chairman, who stepped down as CEO at the turn of the year, said it's been hard to find good deals for years now because the market has shifted in favor of speculators.
sentences.zh: 伯克希尔·哈撒韦公司 (Berkshire Hathaway) 95 岁的董事长在年初辞去了首席执行官一职，他表示，多年来很难找到好的交易，因为市场已经转向有利于投机者。

blocks.html_en: He said that "humans love to gamble so much, there's more money in actually cultivating gamblers than there is in cultivating investors."

sentences.en: He said that "humans love to gamble so much, there's more money in actually cultivating gamblers than there is in cultivating investors."
sentences.zh: 他说，“人类太爱赌博了，培养赌徒的钱比培养投资者的钱还要多。”

blocks.html_en: Business Insider reached out to several of Buffett's close followers for their take on his message. Buffett's secretary didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

sentences.en: Business Insider reached out to several of Buffett's close followers for their take on his message.
Buffett's secretary didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

blocks.html_en: "Buffett is warning investors — not to avoid AI, but to be careful when speculation becomes the dominant force setting prices," Adam Schwartz, the chief investor of Black Bear Value Partners, told Business Insider.

sentences.en: "Buffett is warning investors — not to avoid AI, but to be careful when speculation becomes the dominant force setting prices," Adam Schwartz, the chief investor of Black Bear Value Partners, told Business Insider.
sentences.zh: Black Bear Value Partners 的首席投资者 Adam Schwartz 告诉《商业内幕》：“巴菲特警告投资者，不要回避人工智能，而是当投机成为决定价格的主导力量时要小心。”

blocks.html_en: The business icon has "always believed that eventually fundamentals matter, even if they don't seem to for long stretches," Schwartz continued, adding that investor capital is "chasing excitement instead of cash flows" in the AI era.

sentences.en: The business icon has "always believed that eventually fundamentals matter, even if they don't seem to for long stretches," Schwartz continued, adding that investor capital is "chasing excitement instead of cash flows" in the AI era.
sentences.zh: 施瓦茨继续说道，这位商业偶像“始终相信基本面最终很重要，即使它们似乎不会持续很长时间”。他补充说，在人工智能时代，投资者资本正在“追逐兴奋而不是现金流”。

blocks.html_en: David Kass, a finance professor at the University of Maryland who blogs about Berkshire, told Business Insider that Buffett does seem "concerned" by "troubling" trends in markets such as growing use of derivatives and shrinking time horizons.

sentences.en: David Kass, a finance professor at the University of Maryland who blogs about Berkshire, told Business Insider that Buffett does seem "concerned" by "troubling" trends in markets such as growing use of derivatives and shrinking time horizons.
sentences.zh: 马里兰大学金融学教授大卫·卡斯（David Kass）在博客中介绍了伯克希尔哈撒韦公司，他告诉《商业内幕》，巴菲特似乎确实对市场中“令人不安的”趋势感到“担忧”，例如衍生品使用的增加和时间范围的缩短。

blocks.html_en: But Kass noted that Buffett's "extreme caution" — Berkshire had a record $380 billion cash pile at the end of March — has stopped him from capitalizing on a "sharply rising equity market over the past few years."

sentences.en: But Kass noted that Buffett's "extreme caution" — Berkshire had a record $380 billion cash pile at the end of March — has stopped him from capitalizing on a "sharply rising equity market over the past few years."
sentences.zh: 但卡斯指出，巴菲特的“极度谨慎”——伯克希尔哈撒韦公司截至 3 月底拥有创纪录的 3800 亿美元现金储备——阻止了他利用“过去几年急剧上涨的股市”。

blocks.html_en: The Alphabet puzzle

sentences.en: The Alphabet puzzle
sentences.zh: 字母谜题

blocks.html_en: Buffett flagged during the interview how so-called hyperscalers such as Meta, Microsoft, and Alphabet are spending hundreds of billions of dollars on microchips, data centers, and other infrastructure to win the AI race.

sentences.en: Buffett flagged during the interview how so-called hyperscalers such as Meta, Microsoft, and Alphabet are spending hundreds of billions of dollars on microchips, data centers, and other infrastructure to win the AI race.
sentences.zh: 巴菲特在采访中指出，Meta、微软和 Alphabet 等所谓的超大规模企业如何在微芯片、数据中心和其他基础设施上花费数千亿美元，以赢得人工智能竞赛。

blocks.html_en: "That's real money," he said. "That's the game they're playing now. They weren't playing that game with computer software."

sentences.en: "That's real money," he said.
"That's the game they're playing now.

blocks.html_en: Buffett seemed "troubled" by those huge outlays, Kass said, likely because they're eroding cash flows that previously funded stock buybacks, compelling the companies to raise external capital, and raising the prospect they'll spend a ton of money with little to show for it.

sentences.en: Buffett seemed "troubled" by those huge outlays, Kass said, likely because they're eroding cash flows that previously funded stock buybacks, compelling the companies to raise external capital, and raising the prospect they'll spend a ton of money with little to show for it.
sentences.zh: 卡斯表示，巴菲特似乎对这些巨额支出感到“困扰”，可能是因为它们侵蚀了之前为股票回购提供资金的现金流，迫使公司筹集外部资金，并增加了他们花费大量资金却收效甚微的可能性。

blocks.html_en: Yet Buffett also revealed to CNBC that he decided Berkshire should invest in Alphabet last year. Berkshire ramped up that position to nearly 58 million shares during the nine months ended March 31, securing a stake worth $20.5 billion today.

That has made Alphabet the third-biggest holding in Berkshire's stock portfolio after Apple and American Express, overtaking Coca-Cola.

sentences.zh: 巴菲特在谈到 Alphabet 时表示：“我认为，从记录来看，他们比通过华尔街销售的 90% 或 95% 的产品更有可能成为赢家。”

blocks.html_en: "To make a bet on Google, I think you need to have some sense of the returns on these investments," Gardner said, but "Buffett didn't seem to have a strong view" on that front.

sentences.zh: 加德纳说：“要押注谷歌，我认为你需要对这些投资的回报有一定的了解。”但“巴菲特似乎在这方面没有强烈的看法”。

blocks.html_en: Buffett might think Alphabet can pare its spending if its AI investments aren't paying off, and "return to gushing cash again, which makes it an attractive investment," Gardner said. "I don't know!"

sentences.en: Buffett might think Alphabet can pare its spending if its AI investments aren't paying off, and "return to gushing cash again, which makes it an attractive investment," Gardner said.

blocks.html_en: Whatever Buffett sees in Alphabet, he's picking his spots carefully in a red-hot market, and signaling other investors should do the same.

sentences.zh: 无论巴菲特对 Alphabet 有何看法，他都在炙手可热的市场中谨慎选择自己的位置，并暗示其他投资者也应该这样做。

• Alphabet

sentences.en: Alphabet
sentences.zh: 字母

content_html_zh:
By Theron Mohamed You're currently following this author! 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

沃伦·巴菲特很担心。

这位传奇投资者在周三罕见地接受 CNBC 采访时，痛斥了市场上鲁莽的投机行为以及人工智能建设的巨大成本。

“当每个人都喜欢赌博时，很难找到价值，”这位以纪律严明着称的便宜货猎人说。

伯克希尔·哈撒韦公司 (Berkshire Hathaway) 95 岁的董事长在年初辞去了首席执行官一职，他表示，多年来很难找到好的交易，因为市场已经转向有利于投机者。

他说，“人类太爱赌博了，培养赌徒的钱比培养投资者的钱还要多。”

Business Insider reached out to several of Buffett's close followers for their take on his message. Buffett's secretary didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

Black Bear Value Partners 的首席投资者 Adam Schwartz 告诉《商业内幕》：“巴菲特警告投资者，不要回避人工智能，而是当投机成为决定价格的主导力量时要小心。”

施瓦茨继续说道，这位商业偶像“始终相信基本面最终很重要，即使它们似乎不会持续很长时间”。他补充说，在人工智能时代，投资者资本正在“追逐兴奋而不是现金流”。

马里兰大学金融学教授大卫·卡斯（David Kass）在博客中介绍了伯克希尔哈撒韦公司，他告诉《商业内幕》，巴菲特似乎确实对市场中“令人不安的”趋势感到“担忧”，例如衍生品使用的增加和时间范围的缩短。

但卡斯指出，巴菲特的“极度谨慎”——伯克希尔哈撒韦公司截至 3 月底拥有创纪录的 3800 亿美元现金储备——阻止了他利用“过去几年急剧上涨的股市”。

字母谜题

巴菲特在采访中指出，Meta、微软和 Alphabet 等所谓的超大规模企业如何在微芯片、数据中心和其他基础设施上花费数千亿美元，以赢得人工智能竞赛。

"That's real money," he said. "That's the game they're playing now. They weren't playing that game with computer software."

卡斯表示，巴菲特似乎对这些巨额支出感到“困扰”，可能是因为它们侵蚀了之前为股票回购提供资金的现金流，迫使公司筹集外部资金，并增加了他们花费大量资金却收效甚微的可能性。

Yet Buffett also revealed to CNBC that he decided Berkshire should invest in Alphabet last year. Berkshire ramped up that position to nearly 58 million shares during the nine months ended March 31, securing a stake worth $20.5 billion today.

Berkshire — led by Buffett's successor as CEO, Greg Abel — invested another $10 billion in Alphabet in a private placement in June. The unexpected deal boosted the size of its wager to nearly $31 billion at Thursday's close, assuming no changes have been made. That has made Alphabet the third-biggest holding in Berkshire's stock portfolio after Apple and American Express, overtaking Coca-Cola.

巴菲特在谈到 Alphabet 时表示：“我认为，从记录来看，他们比通过华尔街销售的 90% 或 95% 的产品更有可能成为赢家。”

《巴菲特的早期投资》一书的作者布雷特·加德纳 (Brett Gardner) 告诉《商业内幕》，鉴于他对人工智能建设成本的担忧，他对巴菲特投资 Alphabet 感到“有点困惑”。

加德纳说：“要押注谷歌，我认为你需要对这些投资的回报有一定的了解。”但“巴菲特似乎在这方面没有强烈的看法”。

Buffett might think Alphabet can pare its spending if its AI investments aren't paying off, and "return to gushing cash again, which makes it an attractive investment," Gardner said. "I don't know!"

无论巴菲特对 Alphabet 有何看法，他都在炙手可热的市场中谨慎选择自己的位置，并暗示其他投资者也应该这样做。

阅读下一篇

Theron Mohamed You're currently following this author! 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

Theron Mohamed is a London-based correspondent on the Trending team at Business Insider. His coverage spans finance, investing, wealth, markets, and the economy.Theron joined BI in 2019 as a reporter at Markets Insider and rose to the rank of correspondent before moving to the Trending team in 2024. He previously covered tech, media, and telecom stocks for Investors Chronicle magazine and had a brief stint on the Financial Times' Data team. He interned at the Wall Street Journal in New York where he primarily wrote for Heard on the Street.Theron has freelanced for The Independent, The Telegraph, WIRED, and several smaller publications. He holds an undergraduate degree in geography from the London School of Economics, and a master's degree in journalism from Columbia University.Theron often covers Warren Buffett, Michael Burry, Jeremy Grantham and other top-flight investors. He also writes about the world's wealthiest people and shares financial advice from all manner of rich and successful people.Email Theron at tmohamed@businessinsider.com and follow him on X @theron_mohamed.Expertise
